en-us.html:
"Many things lie between us - shadowy mountains and sounding sea."

Story

I circle my opponent under Bedivere's watchful eye. And this challenger is no ordinary foot soldier – it's Achilles, a veteran of several wars and hero of legendary tales. The Eidolon's copper cuirass shines in the setting sun, and the tip of his lance glimmers in the twilight glow. I carefully monitor every one of his movements, searching for the slightest flaw. But that's easier said than done. Even though he's set aside his helmet and shield, the fearsome reach of his weapon is danger enough on its own. To trip him up and outmatch him with my jian, I'll need to reduce the distance between us and get inside his guard. But how? Suddenly, he strikes. I block at the last second, rotating my wrist slightly to attempt a disruption of my own.

But as I prepare to hit him, his fist clips me on the jaw and I stumble back, realizing he's dropped his weapon to more easily take me with his bare fists. I stagger, then roll to get myself back on guard. But Achilles is faster. Catching his lance on his foot, he launches it in the air to grab it, then spins it around while taking a step toward me. I dodge the shaft that almost hits me, but not the kick that lands right in my ribs. I plant my weapon in the ground to avoid falling, struggling with all my might not to give in to the panic from my gasping breath and spinning vision. When I started the fight, I had hoped I could exploit his heel, but I didn't know it would be so hard to reach.

blocks.text: "Many things lie between us - shadowy mountains and sounding sea."

blocks.title: Story
paragraphs: I circle my opponent under Bedivere's watchful eye. And this challenger is no ordinary foot soldier – it's Achilles, a veteran of several wars and hero of legendary tales. The Eidolon's copper cuirass shines in the setting sun, and the tip of his lance glimmers in the twilight glow. I carefully monitor every one of his movements, searching for the slightest flaw. But that's easier said than done. Even though he's set aside his helmet and shield, the fearsome reach of his weapon is danger enough on its own. To trip him up and outmatch him with my jian, I'll need to reduce the distance between us and get inside his guard. But how? Suddenly, he strikes. I block at the last second, rotating my wrist slightly to attempt a disruption of my own.

But as I prepare to hit him, his fist clips me on the jaw and I stumble back, realizing he's dropped his weapon to more easily take me with his bare fists. I stagger, then roll to get myself back on guard. But Achilles is faster. Catching his lance on his foot, he launches it in the air to grab it, then spins it around while taking a step toward me. I dodge the shaft that almost hits me, but not the kick that lands right in my ribs. fr-fr.html:
« Une mer tumultueuse et des montagnes ombragées d'arbres nous séparent. »

Histoire

Je tourne autour de mon adversaire, sous l'œil attentif de Bedivere. Celui qui me fait face n'est pas le premier venu. Il s'agit d'Achille, vétéran de plusieurs guerres et héros d'épopées légendaires. La cuirasse cuivrée de l'Eidolon luit sous le soleil déclinant, et la pointe de sa lance étincelle dans la clarté crépusculaire. Je suis attentif au moindre de ses gestes, cherchant la plus petite faille. Mais c'est plus facile à dire qu'à faire. Même s'il a laissé de côté son casque et son bouclier, l'allonge de son arme constitue à elle seule un danger. Pour pouvoir le bousculer et le mettre en défaut avec mon jian, il va me falloir réduire la distance et rentrer à l'intérieur de sa garde. Mais comment ? Brusquement, il frappe. Je pare au dernier moment, effectuant une légère rotation de poignet pour tenter moi-même une incartade.

Mais alors que j'arme mon coup, son poing me cueille au niveau de la mâchoire et je bascule en arrière, réalisant qu'il a lâché son arme pour mieux me recevoir avec ses poings nus. Je titube, effectue une roulade pour me remettre en garde. Mais Achille a été le plus rapide. Réceptionnant sa lance sur son pied, il la propulse en l'air pour la saisir, puis la fait virevolter en faisant un pas dans ma direction. J'esquive le coup de manche qui m'était destiné, mais pas le coup de pied qui me percute les côtes de plein fouet. Je plante mon arme dans le sol pour ne pas chuter, luttant de toute ma volonté pour ne pas céder à la panique de mon souffle coupé ou de ma vision encore flottante. En entamant le combat, j'avais espoir de pouvoir exploiter son talon, mais je ne me doutais pas que ce dernier allait être aussi difficile à atteindre.

blocks.text: « Une mer tumultueuse et des montagnes ombragées d'arbres nous séparent. »
